value.found_quote: Branching creates a copy of the conversation so far and switches you into it, leaving the original intact. Use it to try a different approach without losing the path you were on... From the command line, combine `--continue` or `--resume` with `--fork-session`
value.rationale: The page explicitly documents both resuming and branching (forking) previous sessions. The "Resume a session" section covers continuing a previous session via `--continue`, `--resume`, and `/resume` commands. The "Branch a session" section covers forking/branching a previous conversation to try a different approach. These together substantiate the claim of "Resume / fork session (Continue or branch a previous session.)" as active, generally available features of Claude Code.
